MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for (petitioner) Barbara DeVries.

The court stated its concern regarding the weekly $500.00 in cash to the beneficiary. Mr. Siracusa reported that this is a difficult/unusual beneficiary to deal with, Ms. DeVries has tried to find housing for him so she could pay those funds directly, Stephen Danekas is homeless and will not accept housing, feels that reducing the weekly amount would bring hostility from Stephen and states that Stephen seems to be able to handle the money. The court suggested food cards instead of cash and asked if there was information regarding the $10,000.00. Mr. Siracusa stated that he was not sure of the possibility of food cards, that they have no idea what happened to the $10,000,00 but do not think Stephen is being taken advantage of. Mr. Siracusa stated that proposed successor trustee Mr. Bautista is a professional fiduciary but does not actually know him, that Ms. DeVries is not taking additional fees for this account period, that there is nothing unusual in this accounting period and that Ms. DeVries can submit a final accounting directly to the successor trustee. Off Calendar Jul-06-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Barbara DeVries (Petitioner/Trustee). Russell Rowen for and with Julia Danekas (Beneficiary). Joan Rodman for Mark Danekas (Beneficiary). Stephen Gould for and with Sally Danekas (Beneficiary).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to making/not making a non pro rata distribution in lieu of interests in the real property, outright distribution of the property, selling the property/not selling the property, the Court not having the right to force the Parties to enter into an LLC, distributing the property in kind as TIC (Tenants In Common), etc. Counsel is not requesting an evidentiary hearing on either petition for instructions. However, if in a situation of an LLC, Mr. Rowen will request an evidentiary hearing on the terms of the LLC. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court denied the Trustee's petition for instructions. Julia's petition is granted as to the sale of the property. The Court will order a settlement conference where the Parties can have an opportunity prior to the sale to meet with a Judge to see if they can reach an understanding. The Probate Examiner will inform the Parties as to who the Judge will be and the Judge's Clerk's name and telephone number for Counsel to contact the Clerk for a date and time. Mr. Rowen will prepare and submit an order to all Counsel. Mr. Siracusa will also prepare an order as well.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-26-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Darin Judd for Julia Danekas. Stephen Gould for Sally Danekas (Beneficiary). Joan Rodman for Mark Danekas (Beneficiary).

The Court stated that there were three issues: Sally's petition to be reimbursed, two accounting petitions, and the petition to remove Julia. In addition, the Court stated that it was inclined to grant the petition for instructions. Mr. Gould stated that the apartment was rented out and presented argument to support his position. Ms. Rodman stated that her client was ok with the request. Mr. Judd stated that this was not a proper petition for the Court, that the fees to fix the apartments were set at $10,000, that $17,000 in improvements were done but that is not why the apartment could be rented for more money, that this is a creditor's claim, and that if it is a creditor's claim it should be filed as one. The Court addressd the accountings. Mr. Judd stated that the reason counsel could not come up with a date was due to his unavailability; he was in trial. The Court stated that it needed an account that dealt with Turk Street, that attorney time sheets are needed, and that Mr. Judd should file his accounting and give notice. In addition, the Court stated that it wanted the petition to remove Julia (currently set for May 12, 2009) heard after the accountings on a Monday. Mr. Judd stated that he could file the accounting by Friday. The accounting petitions and the petition to remove Julia were continued to June 15, 2009. The Court took the petition for instructions under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Petition For Appointment Of Trustee Of The Stephen Allen Danekas Trust, A Subtrust Created Under The N. Arden Danekas Living Trust
Off Calendar Jun-16-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Darin Judd for Julia Danekas (Trustee). Joan Rodman for Mark Danekas (Objector). Stephen Gould for and with Sally Danekas (Objector).

Mr. Judd stated that Stephen's trust was to be funded when the property was sold, that it would create a sub trust and that they wanted an independant neutral party to be appointed as Trustee of Stephen's trust. Mr. gould stated that the request for a seperate trustee should be denied. Ms. Rodman stated that her client agreed with Mark. The Court denied the request for appointment of a seperate trustee without prejudice. Mr. Judd requested time to respond to the objections, to respond to the Examiner's notes and stated that they would agree to mediation. Mr. Gould stated that he would mediate, but that he felt that the trustee used the Lion street property improperly as leverage to get the accounting approved and that he wanted to see the property distributed first before going to mediation. After hearing argument, the Court continued the accounting for the Trustee to file an amended account. The request to sell the property was denied wothout prejudice. The request for fees and approval of for preliminary distribution was denied without prejudice. Sally is to receive the personal property she requested. Counsel will work out the issue regarding the assets from the Turk St property.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Brenda Fields CSR#7235. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Neu.
